title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Cough
intro: Herbal teas for cough are a type of remedy made from the leaves, flowers, roots, or stems of plants, used to treat and soothe coughs due to their natural antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. These teas can treat coughs effectively because they contain active compounds that help reduce inflammation in the throat, calm the cough reflex, and boost the immune system. Some popular herbal teas for cough include peppermint, which helps to relax the throat muscles and reduce inflammation; ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the throat; licorice root, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to combat infection; thyme, which has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off germs and bacteria; and eucalyptus, which has decongestant properties that help to ease congestion and coughing. Additionally, other herbal teas like chamomile, slippery elm, and mullein also help to calm the cough and soothe the throat, making them a natural and effective remedy for coughs.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments, particularly those associated with respiratory infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, phenolic acids, and glycosides, help to reduce inflammation, suppress the production of mucus, and boost the immune system, thereby alleviating cough symptoms. By modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, Echinacea purpurea teas provide relief from coughs and other respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and sore throats. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat cough ailments include reduced severity and duration of symptoms, faster recovery, and enhanced overall immune function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and roots.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Add the Echinacea purpurea mixture to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe a cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Journal of family practice", Echinacea purpurea teas for cough may be beneficial for the early treatment of acute upper respiratory infections, with generally positive results found in eight out of nine treatment trials.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat cough ailments, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, headaches, and digestive issues in some individuals. When using Echinacea purpurea teas, take precautions to avoid potential interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and be cautious if you have a history of allergies, autoimmune disorders, or kidney or liver problems.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Take Echinacea purpurea tea for 5-7 days to help reduce cough.
Repeat as needed, but not for more than 2 weeks in a row.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The triterpenoid saponins present in this herbal preparation, particularly glycyrrhizin, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cough symptoms by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Glycyrrhiza glabra teas also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that possess antioxidant properties, which help to protect the respiratory tract from oxidative stress and promote healing. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat cough ailments include rapid relief from symptoms, reduced risk of complications, and a non-addictive and gentle approach to managing respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Heat the water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day for cough relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Mini reviews in medicinal chemistry", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for cough may provide symptomatic relief due to its pharmacological actions, which include modulating the immune system, inhibiting virus growth, producing anti-inflammatory activity, and inactivating viruses.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be effective in treating cough ailments, but they may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, low potassium levels, and electrolyte imbalance, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, individuals should be cautious of their overall fluid intake, as excessive consumption may lead to dehydration, and monitor their blood pressure regularly to avoid potential cardiovascular complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 2-3 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra per cup.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra per 8 oz of water.
Drink tea for 5-7 days to see improvement in cough.
Consult doctor before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for chronic cough.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been used to treat cough ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, possess analgesic and expectorant properties that help to reduce inflammation and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel. By reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of excess mucus, Zingiber officinale teas help to alleviate cough symptoms, providing relief from discomfort and congestion. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat cough ailments include natural pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved respiratory function, making it a popular herbal remedy for cough relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale (Ginger) root.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the 1 tablespoon of dried Ginger root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while warm to help soothe a cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Recent patents on biotechnology", Zingiber officinale teas have potential for adjuvant symptomatic therapy in respiratory conditions, such as cough.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be an effective remedy for cough ailments, but consuming it regularly may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with small doses and gradually increase the amount, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and refrain from consuming it with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for cough treatment.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried ginger per cup of boiling water.
Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for 3-5 days to help soothe and calm cough.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage or frequency of usage as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their decongestant, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat cough by loosening and clearing mucus from the respiratory tract, providing quick relief from congestion and coughing. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus teas, including eucalyptol, cineole, and borneol, help to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat cough include rapid relief from symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a natural alternative to over-the-counter medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) and have it ready for adding to the tea.
Place the Eucalyptus leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the Eucalyptus leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea, add the honey (if using), and drink it while it's warm to help soothe a cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas for cough have been reported to be a traditional remedy for cough in Ethiopia, with Eucalyptus globulus being one of the top-cited plant species used to treat respiratory disorders, scoring a frequency of citation of 6.8%.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can cause stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals when consumed to treat cough ailments. To avoid these side effects, it's essential to start with small doses, avoid mixing with other medications, and monitor your body's response closely, especially if you have sensitive skin or a history of allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Eucalyptus leaves per cup.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make the tea.
Drink the tea for 3-5 days to relieve cough symptoms.
Consult a doctor if cough persists or worsens over time.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, a popular herbal remedy, have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their soothing and expectorant properties. The rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid present in these teas help to reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory issues, making it an effective natural remedy for coughs. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis, including rosmarinic acid and camphor, exhibit anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory activities, which help to ease coughing and congestion. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat cough ailments include reducing the severity of symptoms, promoting relaxation, and providing a natural alternative to conventional cough medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve a cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Tip tarihi arastirmalari = History of medicine studies", Rosmarinus officinalis teas for cough have been used to treat various chest diseases, including coughing, since ancient times.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective remedy for cough ailments due to its expectorant properties, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or over an extended period. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, it is essential to start with small doses, avoid taking it with other medications or stimulants, and be cautious when consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Rosmarinus officinalis tea for cough.
Drink 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea 3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves per cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and drink warm tea.
Continue for 3-5 days or as directed by doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, commonly known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat the cough ailment due to their soothing and calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, providing quick relief from coughing. The bioactive constituents, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, present in peppermint tea have been found to possess expectorant properties, helping to loosen mucus and promote its expulsion from the lungs. The benefits of peppermint tea in treating cough include its ability to alleviate symptoms, promote relaxation, and provide a natural remedy for respiratory issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Mentha x piperita (peppermint) leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of the peppermint leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink while it's warm to help relieve cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", Mentha x piperita teas may be beneficial in treating cough due to its traditional use in treating cold and throat inflammation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be beneficial for treating cough ailments, but they may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with certain medications that can exacerbate the condition. Precautions should be taken when using Mentha x piperita teas, including avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to stomach upset, and being cautious when using it in combination with other herbal remedies or medications that thin the blood.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Mentha x piperita tea 3 times a day.
Use 2 tablespoons of dried leaves or 3 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Steep in hot water for 5-7 minutes or as needed.
Drink tea after meals or before bedtime for better relief.
Continue for 3-5 days or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat cough by reducing inflammation in the throat, relieving congestion, and calming the nervous system, thus providing relief from coughing. Bioactive constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, found in Lavandula angustifolia, contribute to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat cough include reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and a soothing effect on the nervous system, making it a popular natural remedy for cough relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Remove the boiling water from heat and add the gathered Lavandula angustifolia leaves.
Steep the leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to release.
Strain the tea and let it cool down before drinking. Drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day for cough relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to help alleviate cough ailments, but possible side effects include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to the essential oils' impact on the digestive system and nervous system. Precautions include starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before bedtime as it may interfere with sleep, and monitoring for allergic reactions such as skin irritation or respiratory issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea, 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain before drinking.
Take 1-2 cups per day, depending on cough severity.
Continue for 3-5 days or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and combat bacterial infections that can cause coughing. By acting as an expectorant, thyme tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from coughing and congestion. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat cough ailments include its natural and non-addictive properties, making it a popular alternative to over-the-counter cough medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Remove the pot from heat and add the Thymus vulgaris leaves to the water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves from the water.
Drink the tea hot or let it cool down, and repeat as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Wiadomosci lekarskie (Warsaw, Poland : 1960)", Thymus vulgaris teas for cough are effective due to the spasmolytic, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, and antioxidant properties of thyme, particularly thymol contained in the volatile thyme oil.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat cough ailments, but its use may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and skin irritation in some individuals. When using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat cough, it is essential to take precautions, such as drinking plenty of water to avoid dehydration, avoiding consumption in children and pregnant women, and starting with small amounts to assess tolerance.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar to soothe the throat.
Repeat the tea drink for 5-7 days to see improvements.
Use this remedy for adults and children over 6 years old.
Consult a doctor if cough persists or worsens over time.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the throat and reduce irritation. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. By reducing inflammation and combating infections, Salvia officinalis teas help to alleviate cough symptoms, promote relaxation, and improve overall respiratory health. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat cough ailments include natural relief from symptoms, reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, and improved quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the leaves to release their flavor and benefits.
Strain the liquid into another cup to remove the leaves. Discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe a cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "European journal of medical research", Salvia officinalis teas for cough may be beneficial as a 15 % sage spray was significantly superior to placebo for reducing throat pain intensity score within the first two hours after first administration.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat cough ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, take small amounts, start with a low dose, and be cautious of the steeping time to avoid excessive thujone, a compound that may cause adverse effects in high concentrations.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea 3 times a day as needed.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried Salvia officinalis per cup.
Gradually increase to 2 tablespoons per cup if needed.
Continue for 3-5 days for best results on cough relief.
Consult a doctor if cough persists or worsens over time.

To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, it is essential to be mindful of interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and to monitor blood pressure, as excessive consumption may lead to a drop in blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas, also known as wild thyme teas, have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including thymol and carvacrol, help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thus providing relief from coughing. The anti-inflammatory properties of Thymus serpyllum teas also help to soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By using Thymus serpyllum teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and effective treatment for cough ailments, without the risk of side effects associated with conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Thymus serpyllum leaves.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe a cough.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to help calm a sore throat.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to treat cough ailments, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or digestive issues. When using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat cough, it's essential to take precautions such as starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and monitoring body temperature, as excessive consumption may lead to symptoms like fever, rapid heartbeat, or even interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Pimpinella anisum teas have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to its expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and coughing. The bioactive constituents, including anethole and limonene, exhibit anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, making it easier to expel mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract. This herbal preparation helps to soothe the throat and reduce coughing spasms, providing relief from cough and cold symptoms. The benefits of Pimpinella anisum teas include their natural and non-addictive nature, making them a popular choice for those seeking alternative remedies for cough treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of Pimpinella anisum seeds and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Pimpinella anisum seeds to a cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to combine.
Strain the mixture into another cup to remove the seeds.
Drink the Pimpinella anisum tea hot, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe a cough.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Pimpinella anisum teas can be used to treat the cough ailment, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea, especially when consumed in large quantities. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using Pimpinella anisum teas if you have a history of stomach problems or sensitive digestive system.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Foeniculum vulgare, including anethole, limonene, and fenchone, help to ease coughs by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and relieving spasms. This herbal preparation works by thinning mucus, reducing congestion, and promoting relaxation, ultimately providing relief from coughs and other respiratory symptoms. Regular consumption of Foeniculum vulgare teas may also help to boost the immune system and prevent the recurrence of cough ailments.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Foeniculum vulgare leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Foeniculum vulgare leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea hot or warm, sweetened with honey if needed, 2-3 times a day to help relieve cough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Fortschritte der Medizin, Foeniculum vulgare teas for cough demonstrated an increase in the transport rate of mucociliar activity, ranking third in effectiveness among the components of the cough tea.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be an effective remedy for soothing a cough, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Foeniculum vulgare teas to treat a cough, it's essential to take precautions, such as drinking plenty of water to avoid dehydration, monitoring blood sugar levels, and being cautious if you have a history of bleeding disorders, as the tea may increase bleeding risk.

preparations.herb_desc: Verbena officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to their soothing and expectorant properties, which help to calm the respiratory tract and facilitate the removal of mucus. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that contribute to its therapeutic effects. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the airways, Verbena officinalis teas help to alleviate coughing and make breathing easier. The benefits of using these teas include relief from coughing fits, reduced congestion, and a soothing effect on the throat, making them a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Verbena officinalis leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the Verbena leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Drink the tea as needed to help soothe a cough.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Verbena officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating cough ailments, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and stomach discomfort, especially when consumed in large quantities or over an extended period. When using Verbena officinalis teas for cough treatment, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small amounts to assess tolerance, avoiding it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and being mindful of potential interactions with other medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as wild marjoram or oregano tea, have been traditionally used to treat cough ailments due to its expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as carvacrol, rosmarinic acid, and thymol, which exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that help soothe the throat and reduce coughing. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause infections, Origanum vulgare teas help alleviate cough symptoms, making it a natural remedy for respiratory issues. The benefits of using Origanum vulgare teas to treat cough ailments include reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, fewer side effects, and a more effective treatment approach for chronic cough conditions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Origanum vulgare leaves and flowers. You can find them in health stores or grow them in your garden.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Remove the pot from the heat and add 1/4 cup of the Origanum vulgare leaves and flowers to the water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe a cough. You can add honey to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas for cough have high antioxidant properties and phenolic compounds content, specifically having a Trolox equivalent antioxidant capacity (TEAC) of 5.87+/-0.2mM and total phenolics content of 1653.61+/-11.52 microM.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat cough ailments, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Origanum vulgare teas to treat cough, it is recommended to take precautions, such as starting with small amounts and gradually increasing the dose, to minimize the risk of side effects, and to avoid using it for extended periods of time.

preparation_study: According to Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica, Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat cough due to its effects in clearing heat and detoxifying, and invigorating Qi.
intro_study: According to "Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania)", teas for cough are used to relieve dry, tiresome cough by covering the oral and throat mucosa with mucilage or a soothing secretion produced by the active substances in medicinal herbs.
